headlines.title: Sen. Cory Booker says people should be ‘very worried’ about Trump and Israel upending peace
headlines.description: Sen. Cory Booker criticized President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, calling them both ‘criminal” leaders.

headlines.title: Trump blames vandals for Reflecting Pool problems and says it may need to be drained again
headlines.description: WASHINGTON — President Donald Trump said Saturday that multiple people had been arrested in connection with alleged vandalism of the Reflecting Pool on the National Mall, including an Olympic canoeist, as the administration’s $14 million project to renovate the site has faced multiple setbacks

headlines.title: Blue paint seen chipping off Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool after algae turn it green
headlines.description: President Donald Trump’s makeover of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool ahead of America’s 250th anniversary celebrations is not going according to plan.

headlines.title: Iran peace talks have been chaotic. But confusion has only grown since the MOU was signed.
headlines.description: WASHINGTON — The effort by the U.S. and Iran to negotiate an end to the war has been defined by dramatic fits and starts and punctuated by missile strikes and naval blockades.

headlines.title: How Does Trump’s Iran Deal Compare to Obama’s From 2015?
headlines.description: President Donald Trump signed a “memorandum of understanding” with Iran to wind-down four months of war, but critics, including politicians and media figures who typically support the president, say it concedes too much to a radical Iranian regime. So how does it stack up with the Obama administration’s 2015 deal with Iran which Trump attacked on the campaign trail and then repealed as president? NBC’s Keir Simmons reports in this week’s Sunday Focus.

headlines.title: Trump Threatens to Arrest Anyone Who Vandalizes Reflecting Pool
headlines.description: President Donald Trump is threatening years in jail for anyone who vandalizes the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C. On Friday, U.S. Park police arrested Olympic canoeist David Hearn, charging him with destruction of government property after he reached into the water to touch a piece of the peeling paint. Hearn denies the charge. NBC’s Gary Grumbach reports for Sunday TODAY.

headlines.title: Trump Faces Backlash From Allies Over His Handling of Iran War
headlines.description: NBC’s Garrett Haake, filling in for Kristen Welker as Moderator of Meet the Press, joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to discuss the reaction from Americans and some of President Donald Trump’s allies to his handling of the Iran war. “Only about one-third of the country say they support how the President has handled the war so far. He needs the country to be with him on how to handle the peace, or else this could be a disaster for Republicans in the midterms,” Garrett says.

headlines.title: U.S. deportation tracker: Counting arrests, detentions and border crossings
headlines.description: President Donald Trump came into office promising the largest mass deportation in U.S history, targeting the more than 10 million unauthorized migrants living in the United States.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Americans believe they aren’t being taught enough about democracy
headlines.description: For Thomas Jefferson, James Madison and other bold-face names struggling to bring about a new nation, an educated and informed citizenry was essential to the success of the American experiment.

headlines.title: Trump endorses a second candidate in the South Carolina governor’s race
headlines.description: President Donald Trump is now throwing his support behind both of the remaining Republicans in the South Carolina governor’s race as he seeks to avoid the possibility of another primary setback this month
